title: rinorea paradoxa
intro: Rinorea paradoxa, also known as the "Sarangot" or "Rinorea", is a medicinal plant species found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It is a type of climbing vine that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds. Rinorea paradoxa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, decoction, and ointment, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
